findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\].*(hopfIdeal|pointsHom)|theorem hopfIdeal_toIdeal|theorem toAdd_pointsHom|theorem mem_range_pointsHom_iff"` hits lines 159, 173, 227, 235, and 261 in this file.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from both definitions and add any missing rewrite/introduction lemmas needed downstream instead of relying on unfolding.
findings.issue: `hopfIdeal` and `pointsHom` are both marked `@[expose]` (lines 159 and 227), exposing proof/definition bodies even though the file already provides characteristic lemmas (`hopfIdeal_toIdeal`, `toAdd_pointsHom`, `pointsHom_injective`, `mem_range_pointsHom_iff`).

findings.evidence: `AdditiveGroup`, `RootsOfUnityGroup`, `SplitTorus`, and `TrivialGroup` expose `pointsMulEquiv`, while `rg -n "namespace AlphaP|pointsMulEquiv|pointsHom|mem_range_pointsHom_iff"` shows `AlphaP` has only `pointsHom`, `pointsHom_injective`, and `mem_range_pointsHom_iff`.
findings.fix: Add a `pointsMulEquiv`/`pointsEquiv` to a bundled subtype or subgroup `{a : Multiplicative A // (Multiplicative.toAdd a)^p = 0}`, with apply/symm/naturality lemmas, and define/use `pointsHom` as its coercion if still needed.
findings.issue: The points API only gives an embedding into `Multiplicative A` plus a range predicate, not the promised identification of `αₚ(A)` with the p-torsion subgroup as a bundled equivalence; this is weaker than the established `pointsMulEquiv` API used by the adjacent group examples.

findings.evidence: Line 184 proves `(mk x)^p = 0` without `@[simp]`; line 261 proves `a ∈ pointsHom.range ↔ (toAdd a)^p = 0` without `@[simp]`, while line 234 already marks the analogous evaluator lemma `toAdd_pointsHom` as simp.
findings.fix: Mark `mk_ι_pow_eq_zero` and `mem_range_pointsHom_iff` as `@[simp]` unless the new bundled equivalence makes the range lemma obsolete.
findings.issue: Characteristic normal-form lemmas are missing automation tags: `mk_ι_pow_eq_zero` and `mem_range_pointsHom_iff` should simplify the generator relation and the range condition, respectively.

summary: The new surface exposes implementation bodies and stops short of the standard points equivalence API for a group-scheme worked example. Add the characteristic equivalence/constructor API and keep implementation details opaque.
